description: Toggles AFK status; anyone sending you a tell gets an automatic reply with your optional message.
Hides your class, level, location, and guild from /who queries.

Changes your target to your target's (or the named player's) target. In macros, follow with a short /pause before /attack or /cast so the new target resolves. Legends adds /assist group|raid|main. /assist on|off toggles auto-attack-on-assist.
Toggles melee autoattack. Macro best practice is the explicit '/attack on' (or off) form so a double-press never leaves you idle mid-fight.
Zone-wide buy/sell channel; the classic WTS/WTB medium in trade hubs.
Toggles automatic ranged-weapon firing at your target. Present in EQ Legends; not part of strict classic-era EQ (rangers held the ranged-attack key).
Puts the item on your cursor into the first free inventory slot. Essential in forage/fishing/summon macros.
Automatically splits looted coin among group members.
Saves and swaps whole weapon sets in one command. EQ Legends feature; classic-era EQ had no bandolier.
Opens your spellbook to the given page.
Opens the bug-report screen (non-functional on P99; behavior in Legends TBD).
Begins the 30-second logout (must be sitting); 'desktop' exits the game entirely.

Casts the spell memorized in the given gem slot. Legends also accepts a spell name (/cast <slot# | spellname>). Repeated /cast lines in a macro act as fizzle insurance -- extra lines fail silently while you are already casting.
Displays the zone your soul is currently bound in.
Clears the current chat window.
Grants the named player permission to drag your corpse with /corpse.
Reports the target's relative level (con color) and faction standing toward you.
Drags your own (or a consented player's) corpse toward you; works within roughly 50 feet. Spammed in macros for corpse dragging.
Declines a duel challenge.
Revokes a player's corpse-dragging consent.
Leaves your group, or (as leader, with a name/target) removes that member.
Performs the named melee discipline. Confirmed in Legends; in classic timelines discs arrived with Kunark-era levels, so availability at Legends' level-50 cap may differ per class.
Executes a skill/ability by hotkey slot. Classic numbering: 1-6 are the Abilities (A) tab, 7-10 the Combat (C) tab. Legends maps 1-10 from the Actions window.
Challenges the targeted player to a duel.
Displays a custom roleplay action ('Soandso <text>') to nearby players. Also /e or a leading colon.
Immediately exits to desktop, leaving your character linkdead in-world briefly. /quit does the same to the main menu.
Opens the feedback screen (unused on emulators; era command).
Opens the text-filtering menu (profanity, spell messages, etc.).
Auto-follows the targeted group member; any movement input breaks it. Only works on group members.
Lists your friends, or adds/removes the named player from the list.
Sends a message to group chat. The default channel for macro announces (heals, mezzes, inc calls).
Sends a message to guild chat; used for raid CH rotations on classic servers without raid chat.
Invites the targeted player to your guild (officer/leader only).
Leaves your guild, or removes the named member (officer/leader only).
Sets the guild message of the day; /get retrieves the current one.
Reports the targeted player's guild affiliation, even if they are anon.
Displays the in-game command help by category.
Hides corpses client-side to reduce clutter and lag; 'none' restores them.
Creates a named hotbutton from the command line that can be dragged to a hotbar.
Blocks communications from the named player; alone, lists your ignores.
Invites the targeted (or named) player to your group.
Toggles your Looking For Group flag, visible in /who searches.
Prints your coordinates as (y, x, z). A staple of camp checks, corpse runs, and log timestamping.
Toggles writing all chat/combat text to a per-character log file -- required for any log-parsing companion app.
Opens the loot window on the targeted corpse without right-clicking.
Transfers group leadership to the named member.
Bard only: auto-twists the listed song gems in order, repeating until stopped. EQ Legends feature -- classic-era EQ had no /melody (manual twisting).
Bard only (Legends): plays songs by priority based on recast time and active buffs rather than a fixed order.
Stops the bard song currently being sung (and halts a melody).
Memorizes an entire saved spell set (Legends convenience command; classic re-memmed gem by gem).
Opens a notepad window for per-character notes.
Zone-wide out-of-character channel; the port-taxi and general-chatter medium.
Exports your inventory or spellbook to a text file in the EQ directory -- companion apps can ingest these.

Macro-only delay in tenths of a second (/pause 10 = 1s; Legends caps at 600 = 60s). On its own line it delays before the NEXT line. Written pause-first on a shared line ('/pause 40, /cast 1'), the other command executes FIRST and the pause runs after it; a line may hold at most two commands and one must be /pause.

syntax: /pet <attack | back off | guard here | follow | sit | as you were | get lost | report health | who leader>
description: Issues pet orders. Key macro verbs: attack (current target), back off (stop fighting), guard here (anchor), follow. Legends adds hold and swarm. Pet commands do not register while you are mid-cast.
Files a request for GM assistance.
Shows character creation date and total time played.
Rolls a visible random number (default 0-100) that nearby players can see -- the loot-roll standard.
Sends a tell to the last person who sent you one.
Toggles roleplay flag: purple name, class/level hidden from /who.
Sends a message to raid chat. Confirmed in Legends; on classic-era servers without a raid window, guilds used /gu or /shout instead.
Targets the last person who sent you a tell -- the raid buffer's best friend, pairing with /tt for reply-and-buff macros. Verify presence in Legends.
Speaks aloud to nearby players and NPCs; plain text lines in a macro are said this way. Used to trigger NPC dialogue keywords.
Warrior only: intercepts a portion of incoming damage for the named player for a short duration; you must stay in melee range of them.
Zone-wide red-text broadcast -- train warnings and raid-critical calls.
Controls how much of player names/titles render over heads.
Sits (meditate/regen) or stands. Sitting mid-macro is the standard 'med after cast' finisher; sitting also stands you up from feign death.
Splits the stated coin from your purse evenly among nearby group members.
Stands up -- breaks meditate, feign death, and camp timers. Used in canni-dance and FD-split macros.
Sets your permanent last name (level 20+ in classic).
Targets the named player, NPC, or corpse. In macros, pair with a short pause before acting on the new target.
Lets single-target group buffs land on non-group targets. Era-uncertain for strict classic (disabled on P99); check Legends behavior.
Sends a private message to the named player.
Sends a tell to your current target -- perfect in buff and rez macros where the target is already selected.
Shows Norrath game time and real-world time.
Legends-only: paints a visual cooldown sweep on the macro's hotbutton (tenths of a second). Cosmetic -- it runs whether or not the commands succeeded.
Enables/disables right-click inspection of other players.
Activates an item's click effect from the command line. EQ Legends feature; classic-era required manually right-clicking the item.
Restricts the 3D world rendering to part of the screen (classic UI trick for chat real estate).

syntax: /who [all] [name | class | level range | zone | lfg | gm]
description: Lists players matching filters; '/who all gm' is the classic GM check, '/who all lfg' finds groupmates.
Runs a /who on your current target, revealing info even for anon/roleplay players.
Yells for help -- nearby players see your name and the direction you are in. Doubles as a locator during trains and corpse runs.
Expands to your current target's name in any spoken/chat macro line, evaluated when that line executes (so lines after /assist name the new target). Case-insensitive (%T works).
Expands to your pet's name.
Target's subjective pronoun (he/she/it).
Target's objective pronoun (him/her/it).
Target's possessive pronoun (his/her/its).
Target's race.
